purpose_statements.purpose_statement: Converts monetary values from cents to dollars format for consistent financial reporting across different database platforms.

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This model creates a comprehensive orders mart by enriching staged order data with summarized item-level metrics and customer ordering sequence information. It provides key business insights including order costs, item counts, food/drink categorization, and customer order history for downstream analytics and reporting.

evidence.description: order_items_summary CTE computes supply cost, product price, and item type counts
compute_booleans CTE joins order data with item summaries and derives food/drink order flags
customer_order_count CTE calculates customer order sequence using window function
Transformation dbt_model reads model.stg_orders, model.order_items and writes model.orders

Import reference to orders

dbt reference stg_orders

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This file defines the order items mart, which contains detailed information about individual items within orders, including their revenue, cost, and categorization as food or drink items. It also includes business logic for calculating various revenue metrics and testing supply cost calculations.

metrics section defines various revenue metrics including simple, ratio, derived, and cumulative metrics
unit_tests section includes test_supply_costs_sum_correctly for validating supply cost calculations

YAML key semantic_models

semantic_models section defines customer grain with measures for orders and lifetime spend
metrics section defines LTV, order count, and derived average order value metrics

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order data by renaming fields, converting cent values to dollar amounts, and truncating timestamps to daily granularity. It serves as a standardized intermediate layer for downstream analytics and reporting on order transactions.

evidence.description: Field renaming (id→order_id, store_id→location_id, customer→customer_id) and currency conversion using {{ cents_to_dollars() }} macro
Timestamp truncation using {{ dbt.date_trunc('day','ordered_at') }}
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_orders and writes model.stg_orders

Import reference to renamed

domains.description: Manages customer data, lifetime value analysis, segmentation, and relationship tracking for business intelligence.
domains.reasoning: All files focus on customer data transformation, enrichment, and analytics with consistent key concepts: customer data, relationship management, lifetime value, and segmentation.

domains.description: Handles product catalog data, pricing, food/drink categorization, and supply chain cost tracking for inventory and menu operations.
domains.reasoning: Files share product data, menu management, supply expenses, cost tracking, and categorization concepts, with clear staging-to-mart flows for product and supply chain data.

domains.description: Processes order transactions, item-level details, revenue calculations, and provides comprehensive order analytics for business reporting.
domains.reasoning: All files center on order data, item details, revenue calculation, and order enrichment with strong interdependencies between orders and order items across staging and mart layers.

domains.description: Manages store location data, site information, and tax rate analysis for geographic business intelligence.
domains.reasoning: Files consistently focus on location data, store locations, and tax analysis, forming a complete staging-to-mart pipeline for location-based analytics.

domains.description: Provides foundational data infrastructure including time dimensions, schema management, currency conversion, and project configuration.
domains.reasoning: Files support cross-cutting concerns: currency conversion for financial standardization, schema governance, time-series foundations, source configuration, and project automation tools that serve all business domains.

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This configuration file defines pre-commit hooks that automatically validate and format code before commits are made, ensuring code quality and consistency across the repository.

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This is the main configuration file for a dbt project named 'jaffle_shop' that defines project metadata, resource paths, and model configurations. It specifies database connection settings, directory structures, and materialization strategies for different model types.

Path configurations for models, analyses, tests, seeds, macros and snapshots
Model materialization configurations for staging (view) and marts (table)
